name: Republican Party of New Hampshire
subject: NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester Statement on Rockingham District 04 Primary
body:
PRESS RELEASE

For Immediate Release: August 8, 2017

Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310

Statement from NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Rockingham
District 04 Primary

Concord, NH – NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the
following statement regarding the ACLU lawsuit dismissal:

"Republican voters of Auburn, Chester, and Sandown made a solid
choice tonight by nominating James Headd to be their next State
Representative. I’d like to congratulate James and personally
thank him for stepping up and fighting for New Hampshire families
and taxpayers. I’d also like to thanks James Devine and Anthony
Rossignol for running honorable campaigns.

###

But we still have work to do. We need to keep fighting the liberal obstructionists who want to see the people FAIL. President Trump can't take this fight alone. He needs you and me to stand by his side.

name: Ralph Northam
subject: Derek, I need your help to keep Virginia blue
body:
Help Ralph Northam become Virginia's next governor. **Add your name to join Ralph Northam's campaign for governor in Virginia.

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Join-Ralph-Northam1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170809_rn_nd&source=em_20170809_rn_nd")**

Derek --

When I was serving in the Army, treating wounded soldiers during Desert Storm, I never once had a patient stop me before a medical procedure to ask if I was a Democrat or Republican. And I never asked them. It didn't matter -- we were all in it together.

I look at government the same way. When our elected officials work together for the common good, it lifts us all up.

In Virginia, we got a lot done by putting the people we serve ahead of politics. We came together on a bipartisan proposal to ban smoking in Virginia restaurants -- it wasn't easy, but as a doctor, I know it was a meaningful victory for public health. And we worked together to expand health care and benefits for veterans, and take on the crisis of veteran homelessness.

I'm proud of what we've accomplished, but I know there's still more we can do to make sure no Virginian gets left behind. That's why I'm running for Governor -- to keep giving back.

**There isn't much time left in this election -- less than a hundred days. I need your help to win in November so we can keep moving forward in Virginia. Join my campaign today, and let me know I can count on you:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Join-Ralph-Northam1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170809_rn_nd&source=em_20170809_rn_nd")**
Thank you,

Ralph

|

name: Debbie
subject: Internet as we know it is at risk
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| Friend -- it's easy to take the Internet for granted. With just a few clicks we can find clean cooking recipes, chat with friends on social media, and shop at businesses both large and small. For small businesses, the Internet is a lifeline they depend on for success. It allows them to market their products to millions and allows consumers to shop for the best quality and price. **The Internet as we know it could be at risk if Republicans have their way on net neutrality.** During the Obama administration, the FCC passed safeguards to protect net neutrality. These safeguards prevent Internet providers from slowing down or blocking your ability to view certain sites or online content. If Republicans axe these protections, corporations could make it increasingly more difficult to access their competitor's products and sites. **In essence, the Internet would become pay-to-play -- with consumers, small businesses, and startups suffering the consequences.** I am determined to defend the innovation and economic growth that has resulted from our free and open Internet. With your help, we can keep it that way. [_**Add your name and send a message to Republicans and their corporate sponsors: Net neutrality is here to stay.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e20/622b7b5b/4e3b8f0/307bde5b/2419792531/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e170807") Thank you, Debbie |

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: Roadblocks aren’t always who you think
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

It’s not only the GOP. Democrats block progress sometimes, too.

Like on the Electoral College, which actively undermines the will of
the people and legitimate democracy. Despite having the opportunity to
join the National Popular Vote Interstate Compact this year,
Democratic state legislators in Connecticut and Oregon blocked key
legislation.

But LegitAction is pushing for the national popular vote. And pushing
to restore the ability to vote for disenfranchised former felons, and
pushing to end all hyperpartisan gerrymandering, and calling on
Democrats to step down from President Trump’s sham election
commission, and dozens more priorities that go to the heart of our
democratic legitimacy.

With President Trump increasingly undermining the rule of law, America
needs someone looking out for the legitimacy of our democracy. And
right now, that’s us.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/361?t=1002&akid=196%2E356062%2E3pDsLo ]Contribute $3 to LegitAction today to make sure
we have the resources to keep looking out for the legitimacy of our
democracy in the Trump era.

The work LegitAction is doing during this moment is critical to
protecting our democracy. But we know this won’t be the last moment
that matters. We’re bracing for a long fight.

Thank you for standing up for our democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Enough.
body:
#
Team --
On Monday, I was told that the Republican initiated investigation of my emails was formally put to rest -- no wrongdoing, no "there there," everything I've said over and over. But that's not a win.
Because the political hachetmen don't care about the truth. They only care about impressions that damage reputations by any means necessary.
But there are more of us than there are of them!
The only way this is a win for us is if you stand up with me and say "Enough."
To say with me that our campaign is not going anywhere but up.
That we are going to work everyday to break from the old way of doing business.
We are going to do away with barriers that are stacked against our ability to achieve the American dream.
And we are going to destroy the notion that everyday people like you and me don't deserve at seat at the decision-making table.
The only vindication for us is a win.
[**Watch our latest video here, and then chip in to support our campaign:**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32d2/9e00690/4c5620b8/24036dac/2255816525/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-august2017-vid?refcode=em170809-video")
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32d2/9e00690/4c5620b8/24036dac/2255816525/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-august2017-vid?refcode=em170809-video")
[**If you believe in our journey, not smears against us, join in supporting this campaign today.**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32d2/9e00690/4c5620b8/24036dac/2255816525/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-august2017-vid?refcode=em170809-video")
Even $3 so they know you're not with them.
$3 to bring it home for us.
Thank you,
Andrew

subject: Going directly to the people

body:
****
Friend --

The Democrats are claiming that Republicans are out of touch with the American people.

It's an odd claim coming from the Democrats who are facing historic lows -- from state legislatures and governorships to the House, Senate, and White House.

**But without our work getting much attention, the Democrats can claim whatever they want.[That's why we're following President Trump's lead and taking our message directly to the people.](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=MzAxMTIyNjcS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEwMTA0ODU1OQS2&mt=1&rt=0)**

Republicans just want to make people's lives better. Simple as that.

Most recently, we passed landmark legislation to support our veterans. They deserve a pay raise, so we gave them one -- the largest in 8 years. And we expanded the GI bill.

But news like this just isn't getting out. [**Help us spread the word >>**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MzAxMTIyNjcS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEwMTA0ODU1OQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

**The fact is, Democrats love to talk about how Republicans are out of touch with regular Americans.** But it's Republicans who are going to work for regular Americans by fighting for the agenda you voted for in 2016.

We're working for you, Friend.**[If you're fed up with the Democrats' claims and want to get our message out, I hope you'll take a stand today.](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MzAxMTIyNjcS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEwMTA0ODU1OQS2&mt=1&rt=0)**

Thank you,

Paul Ryan

name: Ralph Northam
subject: re: Trump's ally in Virginia
body:
Help Ralph Northam become Virginia's next governor. **Add your name to join Ralph Northam's campaign for governor in Virginia.

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Join-Ralph-Northam1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170809_rn2_nd&source=em_20170809_rn2_nd")**

Derek, Donald Trump couldn't ask for a better ally than Ed Gillespie.

As a longtime corporate lobbyist, Ed Gillespie made his living helping big corporations avoid paying their fair share. (It's no surprise he and Trump get along so well!)

When Trump was threatening the health care of hundreds of thousands of Virginians, Gillespie was nowhere to be found. And when he did finally have something to say about health care, it was to announce that he'd defund Planned Parenthood.

Now, Ed Gillespie is crisscrossing the commonwealth promising to be Donald Trump's man in Richmond. And there's a real chance they could win -- with less than a hundred days left until Election Day, this race is tied.

We've come this far because I've had Democrats like you in my corner, but I need your help today to defeat Trump and Gillespie once and for all.

**Add your name and join my campaign. Together, we can stop the Trump-Gillespie agenda and keep Virginia blue:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Join-Ralph-Northam1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170809_rn2_nd&source=em_20170809_rn2_nd")**
We're in the final sprint. Thanks for helping to get us across the finish line.

Ralph

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Become a citizen co-sponsor of my Medicare-for-all bill
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
I am introducing a Medicare-for-all bill in the coming weeks. It is time to wage a moral and political war against a dysfunctional health care system.
#
Sisters and Brothers -
Last month, we won a very important victory against the Republican plan to kick millions of Americans off their health insurance, a plan that would have raised premiums for seniors and defunded Planned Parenthood.
Now let me be totally clear: if Republicans try again to repeal Obamacare, we will fight them with everything we have. But our ultimate goal is to understand that in the wealthiest country in the history of the world, health care must be a right, not a privilege, for every man, woman and child.
**That is why, in just a few weeks, I am going to introduce a Medicare-for-all, single-payer health care bill in the United States Senate. It is time to wage a moral and political war against a dysfunctional health care system in this country. Say you 're with me:**
[**Add your name as a Citizen Co-Sponsor of my Medicare-for-all, single-payer health care bill. When I introduce the legislation, I want it to be clear that the American people believe health care should be a right in this country.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-sanders-mfa?source=em170809-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-sanders-mfa?source=em170809-full")
Medicare-for-all will mean that people won't die in this country simply because they cannot afford the care they need. It will mean everyone, and every loved one, has the freedom to go to a doctor when they need without going bankrupt.
A Medicare-for-all, single-payer health care system would save hundreds of billions in health care costs a year. The savings for businesses would be astronomical and allow them to compete on equal footing with companies in Europe.
And for the millions of Americans who are currently in jobs they don't like but must stay put because of health care access, they would be free to explore more productive opportunities as they desire.
Right now, our nation spends more than any other on health care with higher costs and worse outcomes in many cases. If we are serious about providing high-quality and affordable health care, the only real solution is a Medicare-for-all, single-payer system that provides health care to **ALL** Americans, including the 50 million uninsured and underinsured in this country.
Before I introduce my legislation, I want to know that you're with me:
[**Add your name as a Citizen Co-Sponsor of my Medicare-for-all, single-payer health care bill.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-sanders-mfa?source=em170809-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-sanders-mfa?source=em170809-full")
The insurance companies may not like this legislation and the drug companies probably won't either. They will try to make words like "NATIONAL HEALTH CARE" sound scary.
But the truth is, for far too long they have been bribing the United States Congress, spending billions of dollars in lobbying and campaign contributions to make sure we maintain a dysfunctional, but profitable, approach to health care. Enough is enough. Now is the time to take them on and do something about it.
The American people are with us on this issue, and there is nothing to be afraid of. Because right now, we already have a very large single-payer system in this country. It's called Medicare and it has succeeded in providing near-universal coverage to Americans over the age of 65 and gets high marks from people who are enrolled in the program.
I believe the American people are ready for a Medicare-for-all, single-payer system that guarantees health care as a right for all Americans. But getting there will require nothing short of a political revolution in this country.
Add your name to say we're in this together.
[**Add your name as a Citizen Co-Sponsor of my Medicare-for-all, single-payer health care bill. When I introduce the legislation, I want it to be clear that the American people believe health care should a right in this country.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-sanders-mfa?source=em170809-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-sanders-mfa?source=em170809-full")
We need a revolution in how health care is provided in this country. My bill will do that. If every other major country on earth can offer health care as a right, then so can the United States of America.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
****[ADD YOUR NAME](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-sanders-mfa?source=em170809-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-sanders-mfa?source=em170809-full")****
** **
** **

name: Jenna Kruse, EMILY's List
subject: Every 👏 single 👏 day 👏
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c658f/24c69fd9/b42951d5/189858ec/4061354705/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE821N_digital_email_20170809_BasicSenateText_inact&source=WQEFE821N_digital_email_20170809_BasicSenateText_inact")
---

Peter,

The health care repeal debate had many core-shaking moments.

One that I can't stop thinking about was when Senator **Mazie Hirono** , recently diagnosed with kidney cancer, took to the Senate floor and made an impassioned plea to her colleagues:
"Where is your compassion? Where is the care that you showed me when I was diagnosed with my illness? ... You showed me your care. You showed me your compassion. Where is that tonight?"
Mazie knows the importance of affordable, comprehensive health care because she's living it. That's why she and the rest of our Senate women stood strong against Trumpcare, refusing to support anything that took health care away from others.

**[Mazie is up for re-election next year, and we need to elect nine other EMILY's List women, too. Donate $3 to support all of them as they support all of us.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c658f/24c69fd9/b42951d5/189858ed/4061354705/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170808_email_Senate10Text?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Mazie+is+up+for+reelection+next+year+and&utm_campaign=WQEFE821N_digital_email_20170809_BasicSenateText_inact&source=WQEFE821N_digital_email_20170809_BasicSenateText_inact")**

In addition to Mazie, our women running in the Senate impress me every day.
**Tammy Baldwin** , the first openly LGBTQ senator, strongly condemned President Trump's proposed ban on transgender people serving in the military.

As the top Democrat on the Senate Committee on Energy and Natural Resources, **Maria Cantwell** is investigating whether the Trump administration is interfering with the work of those combating climate change.

**Dianne Feinstein** , who sits on the Senate Judiciary Committee, is making sure no stone is left unturned in the investigation on Russia's interference in the 2016 election.

**Kirsten Gillibrand** isn't giving up the fight for universal paid family leave -- she's working to build bipartisan support to pass her plan to guarantee 12 weeks paid time off for new parents.

**Amy Klobuchar** has called on the Trump administration to rescind its request for massive amounts of voter data -- including social security numbers and birth dates -- even suggesting that the committee that issued the request go "jump in Lake Superior."

**Claire McCaskill** is running a tough re-election race in a state that President Trump won last year, but she's stood strong against his harmful policies, voting against Trumpcare at every stage. Now, she's using the August recess to work with a bipartisan group of senators to make health care even better and more accessible.

**Jacky Rosen** is our newest addition to this group -- she's taking on Trumpcare's Deciding Vote Dean Heller, the most vulnerable Republican in the Senate. I'm SO excited to watch her take his seat in November. No better woman for the job!

While Kid Rock may be toying with the idea of running for Senate in Michigan, **Debbie Stabenow** is focusing on working for her constituents. She introduced a bill allowing people to buy into Medicare at age 55 -- a major step towards making health care more affordable and accessible.

**Elizabeth Warren** kicked off the year holding Trump nominee Jeff Sessions accountable, and she hasn't stopped there. Last month, she _grilled_ Trump's pick to oversee regulations on Wall Street, criticizing his "30-year career spinning through the revolving door in the private sector." Reports say he was left "visibly shaken."
Our Senate women fight for us every. single. day.

**Let's make sure they have everything they need to head to the Senate again next year. Donate $3 to fight for them:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Senate-Women-Fight](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c658f/24c69fd9/b42951d5/189858ee/4061354705/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170808_email_Senate10Text?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgSenateWomenFight&utm_campaign=WQEFE821N_digital_email_20170809_BasicSenateText_inact&source=WQEFE821N_digital_email_20170809_BasicSenateText_inact")**

Thanks,

Jenna Kruse
Vice President of Research, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c658f/24c69fd9/b42951d5/189858ef/4061354705/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170808_email_Senate10Text?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFE821N_digital_email_20170809_BasicSenateText_inact&source=WQEFE821N_digital_email_20170809_BasicSenateText_inact")
